note: Crafts of the Riven Marches/Stillhold Reach (violence_peace axis). The Edge is the violence-pole tradition (honed conflict, the arts of force); Stillcraft the peace-pole (de-escalation, binding, true truce). Learned by traveling the Reach or training with Marchers/the Stillhold. Both drift the wielder toward their pole; the Held Breath jewel is where they co-fire. The Valley's Mediator's Tongue is a folk-shadow of Stillcraft. axes signed for engine compat. Authored by Aevi (PO), SNG-016.

abilities.name: The Edge
abilities.narrationHints: Violence as a discipline, not a rage — economical, decisive, terrible in its precision. The Marchers do not brawl; they end.
abilities.description: The violence-pole mastery: the martial disciplines honed to an art. Not fury — force made exact.
abilities.notFor: Cruelty (the Edge is decisive, not sadistic — that's a different corruption); winning without cost; anything that must be gentle.

tree.name: The First Cut
tree.grants: Strike with trained decisiveness; read a fight's shape and end it fast; stand where others break.
tree.cannot: Force is force — it makes enemies and consequences; the Edge cannot un-hurt.

tree.name: The Marcher's Calm
tree.grants: Fight many, fight cornered, fight wounded and keep the discipline; disarm and disable without killing when you choose; make your reputation do half the work.
tree.cannot: The calm can slip; every use of the Edge writes on you; a foe past reason isn't disabled by skill alone.

tree.name: The Ender
tree.grants: Meet even the great and the many; end a threat at the scale of a warband; the Edge at full mastery, where a single figure turns a field. The Redline's power in miniature.
tree.cannot: The wielder drifts violence-hot — the Ender who forgets peace becomes the thing the Marches are fleeing from; force at this scale foreclosing on everything it touches.

abilities.name: Stillcraft
abilities.narrationHints: Peace as an act, not an absence — the will to hold a room, a feud, a war from tipping, one breath at a time.
abilities.description: The peace-pole mastery: de-escalation, arbitration, and the making of truces that hold. The full craft the Valley's Mediator's Tongue only shadows.
abilities.notFor: Forcing calm on the unwilling (Stillcraft opens the door, never shoves through it); winning a fight (it prevents them); a peace built on a lie holds only until the lie breaks.

tree.name: The Held Room
tree.grants: Halt a rising conflict before it breaks; read what each side truly needs; make yourself the still point a room organizes around.
tree.cannot: Can't stop violence already loosed; the truly bent-on-blood pass through it.

tree.name: The Binding Truce
tree.grants: Broker truces that HOLD — agreements weighted by your standing; bind two enemies to a peace neither wanted but both can live in; feel a truce start to fray before it snaps.
tree.cannot: A truce is only as true as its terms; brokering a peace over an injustice buys time, not resolution — and Stillcraft knows the difference.

tree.name: The Long Peace
tree.grants: Hold a peace at the scale of peoples — end a generational feud, stand a war down, keep a Held Breath from letting out. The Stillhold's power in miniature; the counter-road to the Ender.
tree.cannot: The wielder drifts peace-hot — and peace at all costs becomes the Stillhold's prison, a stillness that forecloses on justice to keep the quiet. A peace that suppresses is its own violence, delayed.

abilities.name: Read the Fight
abilities.narrationHints: The shape of the coming violence, seen a beat early.
abilities.description: Read a fight's flow, a foe's next move, the moment before it breaks.
abilities.notFor: Winning it for you; non-combat reads.

tree.name: Read the Fight
tree.grants: See a fight's shape and a foe's tell a beat early; know when violence is about to erupt.
tree.cannot: Reads, not wins; the truly wild are harder to call.

tree.name: The Whole Field
tree.grants: Read every fighter at once — who is committed, who is looking for the door, where the line will fail first.
tree.cannot: Reading the fight is not surviving it. The field tells you where it breaks, not how to be elsewhere.

tree.name: Three Moves Ahead
tree.grants: See the fight's shape before it takes it — where this ends, and the two or three ways it could end otherwise.
tree.cannot: Seeing the end does not mean you can choose it. The Marchers say the field always keeps one move back.

abilities.name: Read the Room
abilities.narrationHints: Every unspoken tension in a room, felt at once.
abilities.description: Sense a room's tensions, alliances, and the true temperature under the words.

tree.name: Read the Room
tree.grants: Read the social tensions and true feeling in a gathering; find who's about to break the peace.
tree.cannot: Reads, not calms; the disciplined mask well.

tree.name: The Room's True Weather
tree.grants: Read what a room is actually doing under what it is saying — the alliance forming, the grudge about to speak.
tree.cannot: It reads the room, not the world. What is decided elsewhere arrives regardless of how well you read this table.

tree.name: The Thing Nobody Said
tree.grants: Name the thing everyone in the room is carefully not saying — and know what will happen if it is said.
tree.cannot: Knowing it and saying it are different acts, and the second one is rarely free.

abilities.name: Disarming Strike
abilities.narrationHints: The blade leaves the hand before the hand knows it.
abilities.description: End a threat without ending a life — disarm, disable, drop a foe clean. The Edge's mercy.
abilities.notFor: Killing (this spares); many foes at once.

tree.name: Disarming Strike
tree.grants: Disarm or disable a single foe without killing them.
tree.cannot: One foe; the desperate keep coming.

tree.name: The Emptied Hand
tree.grants: Take the weapon rather than the life — cleanly, from a committed strike, without opening yourself.
tree.cannot: An emptied hand is not an ended fight. Some fighters are worse without the weapon.

tree.name: The Ended Fight
tree.grants: End a fight by taking everything it needed to continue — the weapon, the footing, the will, in one motion.
tree.cannot: It ends the fight, not the enmity. What you disarm today comes back better armed.

abilities.name: The Calming Word
abilities.narrationHints: A voice pitched just so, and the raised fist lowers.
abilities.description: Talk a single person or small moment down from the edge of violence — folk-Stillcraft.

tree.name: The Calming Word
tree.grants: De-escalate one person or a small tense moment before it turns violent.
tree.cannot: Small scale; the set-on-blood pass through it.

tree.name: The Cooling Word
tree.grants: Cool a room off a violence it had already decided on — the raised hand comes down, unsure why.
tree.cannot: It cools the heat, not the cause. What made them raise the hand is still sitting there.

tree.name: The Word That Lands
tree.grants: Say the one thing that makes the violence impossible — the true word, aimed exactly, that they cannot go past.
tree.cannot: You must actually find the true thing. The Stillhold's failure is a beautiful word that is not true, and everyone hears it.

abilities.name: The Stand
abilities.narrationHints: You do not move, and so the line holds.
abilities.description: Hold a position or protect another under assault — the guardian's discipline.
abilities.notFor: Winning ground (this holds it); ranged threats.

tree.name: The Stand
tree.grants: Stand firm protecting a spot or person; absorb an assault and keep the line.
tree.cannot: Holds, doesn't advance; overwhelming force still breaks through.

tree.name: The Held Line
tree.grants: Hold a doorway, a bridge, a breach — and hold it against numbers, for longer than a body should.
tree.cannot: Holding is not winning. The line you hold is still a line you are standing on when it fails.

tree.name: The Line That Holds
tree.grants: The line does not break while you are on it. Others rally to it; it becomes the fact the fight bends around.
tree.cannot: It bends around you — including everything aimed at you. Ask Kesh Ardent what the Edge That Holds has paid.

abilities.name: Broker Truce
abilities.narrationHints: Two enemies, one held agreement, weighted by your word.
abilities.description: Broker a real truce between hostile parties, weighted by your standing — mid-tier Stillcraft.
abilities.notFor: Forcing peace; a peace over an injustice holds only briefly.

tree.name: Broker Truce
tree.grants: Get two hostile parties to a truce that holds while its terms hold.
tree.cannot: Only as true as its terms; injustice buried buys time, not peace.

tree.name: The Kept Truce
tree.grants: Broker a truce that HOLDS — bound to something both sides actually value, not merely agreed to.
tree.cannot: A truce holds until one side's need outgrows what it was bound to. Then it holds nothing.

tree.name: The Binding Peace
tree.grants: Bind a peace so well made that breaking it costs more than the war would — and both sides can see it.
tree.cannot: You must find the thing they both value. Where no such thing exists, the Stillcraft has nothing to offer, and says so.

abilities.name: The Held Breath
abilities.narrationHints: Violence and peace both at a knife's edge, held by will alone.
abilities.description: The braid-craft: hold a moment where violence is total and peace is total in perfect tension — an armed grace.
abilities.notFor: Resolving the tension (this holds it); rest.

tree.name: The Held Breath
tree.grants: Hold a razor's-edge standoff open — maximum threat, maximum restraint — by sheer presence.
tree.cannot: Holds, doesn't end; exhausting; one slip and it looses.

abilities.name: The Last Form
abilities.narrationHints: One form, perfectly read and perfectly answered — the duel ends in a single exchange no one saw complete.
abilities.description: The duel capstone: read a single opponent so completely and strike so precisely that a formal fight ends in one decisive exchange. The Marcher's highest art — the fight already over when it begins.
abilities.notFor: Mass combat (this is 1v1); killing where the forms forbid; beating a foe who won't engage the duel.

tree.name: The Last Form
tree.grants: End a formal duel in a single, undeniable exchange — you have read them fully and answer perfectly.
tree.cannot: Only against one committed opponent; a foe who refuses the form, or a chaos-fighter with no form to read, defeats it.
